title: The First Question
description: The first prototype: one question, four choices, instant feedback, and a short explanation.

dialogue.speaker: T
dialogue.text: At first, the world was only one question.

dialogue.speaker: G
dialogue.text: But one question was enough to prove that the world could answer.

dialogue.speaker: T
dialogue.text: A learner could choose from four paths.

dialogue.speaker: G
dialogue.text: The page could say correct or wrong, then explain why.

dialogue.speaker: T
dialogue.text: One question worked... so the next question was how to organize many.

projectStage.title: Prototype v0: Single-Question Website
projectStage.caption: A recovered fragment from the first working prototype: one question, four choices, feedback, and explanation.

title: The First Categories
description: The questions were organized into logical groups, so learning could be explored one topic at a time.

dialogue.speaker: T
dialogue.text: One question became many.

dialogue.speaker: G
dialogue.text: But many questions without order would become noise.

dialogue.speaker: T
dialogue.text: So we gave them categories.

dialogue.speaker: G
dialogue.text: Fractions, integers, and geometry each became a path of learning.

dialogue.speaker: T
dialogue.text: The paths were clear... but paths asked for a place.

projectStage.title: Prototype v1: Topic Categories
projectStage.caption: A recovered fragment from the stage where questions were classified into topic groups.

title: The First Map
description: The project became a world map, turning learning areas into places students could explore.

dialogue.speaker: T
dialogue.text: Paths asked for a place.

dialogue.speaker: G
dialogue.text: So we drew the first map.

dialogue.speaker: T
dialogue.text: Each topic became a tower on the island.

dialogue.speaker: G
dialogue.text: Students no longer only selected a topic. They explored a world.

dialogue.speaker: T
dialogue.text: A map is only a picture until it has rules.

projectStage.title: Prototype v2: The First Map
projectStage.caption: A living fragment from the stage where topic practice became a map with towers.

title: The First Rules
description: The world gained rules: challenges, progress, key fragments, and consequences.

dialogue.speaker: T
dialogue.text: A map is only a picture until it has rules.

dialogue.speaker: G
dialogue.text: So towers became challenges.

dialogue.speaker: T
dialogue.text: Correct answers restored energy. Wrong answers lowered HP.

dialogue.speaker: G
dialogue.text: Key fragments marked progress, and the center stayed locked.

dialogue.speaker: T
dialogue.text: The world could now be played... but a journey can be lost if the world cannot remember.

projectStage.title: Prototype v3: Game Rules
projectStage.caption: A recovered fragment from the stage where HP, combos, Seal Energy, clearing, and failure became playable rules.

title: The First Memory
description: The world learned to remember progress through Save and return to it through Load.

dialogue.speaker: T
dialogue.text: A journey can be lost if the world cannot remember.

dialogue.speaker: G
dialogue.text: So we gave the world memory.

dialogue.speaker: T
dialogue.text: Save wrote progress into a record.

dialogue.speaker: G
dialogue.text: Load returned the world to a remembered path.

dialogue.speaker: T
dialogue.text: With memory restored, the pieces could become a full journey.

projectStage.title: Prototype v4: Save and Load Memory
projectStage.caption: A recovered fragment from the stage where progress could be saved as JSON and loaded back into the world.

title: The First Journey
description: The project became a complete journey with opening, characters, Chronicles, Time Fragments, final trial, and ending.

dialogue.speaker: T
dialogue.text: At last, the pieces became a journey.

dialogue.speaker: G
dialogue.text: There was a beginning, a guide, records, fragments, trials, and an ending.

dialogue.speaker: T
dialogue.text: Students could enter as a team and meet Sage Tong.

dialogue.speaker: G
dialogue.text: They could recover Chronicles, witness Time Fragments, and face the Creator's Trial.

dialogue.speaker: T
dialogue.text: The demo was complete... and the final trial waited at the center.

projectStage.title: Prototype v5: Complete Narrative Demo
projectStage.caption: A recovered fragment from the complete demo experience: opening, characters, Chronicles, Time Fragments, Creator's Trial, and ending.
